insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Saudi Arabia's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in performance overall. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum, with their performance declining at an average rate of 0.100 minutes, and they were unable to regain their footing after the break. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their level of performance, with no average decline in their momentum. This suggests that Saudi Arabia may need to adjust their strategy when taking hydration breaks, particularly when they're on the front foot, in order to minimize the disruption to their game.
